title: Accelerator Physicist — Proton Beam Systems
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. Our near-term proton-lithium (p-Li) accelerator is the engineering anchor of Phase 1: a clinical BNCT source by day, a particle neutron source for industrial NDE off-clinical-hours.
description: Lead the procurement, integration, and commissioning of ASPL's proton accelerator. Hands-on across vendor evaluation, beam transport and focussing design, target integration, and commissioning through to first beam.
responsibilities: Lead accelerator procurement — vendor evaluation, specification, delivery management
Design beam transport, focussing optics, and beam-diagnostic systems
Integrate the lithium target with the micro-channel cooling system and Beam Shaping Assembly (BSA)
Commission the accelerator through to first beam
Develop operating procedures and training materials for facility staff
Support AERB Category-II licensing with accelerator-specific documentation
Contribute to patent filings on novel beam-system configurations
requirements: PhD in Accelerator Physics, Nuclear Physics, or Beam Physics
3+ years of experience with electrostatic accelerators, LINACs, or cyclotrons
Understanding of target physics — preferably ⁷Li or Be targets
preferred: Experience with BNCT or medical-accelerator systems
Prior AERB or equivalent regulatory engagement experience
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
